5	import (
6		"os/exec"
7		"syscall"
8	)
9	
10	// cmdSetProcessGroup configures cmd so that it and all its children share a
11	// new process group (Setpgid) and are automatically killed when the parent
12	// thread dies (Pdeathsig). This ensures that if the parent worker dies
13	// abnormally (SIGKILL, crash, OOM), the harness child is reaped by the kernel
14	// rather than orphaning to PID 1.
15	//
16	// Pdeathsig is sent when the creating thread dies, not the process, so the
17	// caller must use runtime.LockOSThread around cmd.Start() to prevent Go
18	// runtime from migrating the goroutine to a different OS thread. See
19	// golang.org/issue/27505 for details.
20	//
21	// Note: Pdeathsig is Linux-only. macOS and Windows do not support it; the
22	// graceful shutdown path (cmdKillProcessGroup on context cancellation) serves
23	// as the fallback for those platforms, and the orphan reaper is the final
24	// backstop for already-dead workers.
25	func cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
26		cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{
27			Setpgid:   true,
28			Pdeathsig: syscall.SIGKILL,
29		}
30	}
31	
32	// cmdKillProcessGroup sends SIGKILL to the entire process group of cmd.
33	// Because cmd was started with Setpgid=true, its PID equals its PGID, so
34	// passing -pid kills all descendants that inherited the group.
35	func cmdKillProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
36		if cmd.Process == nil {
37			return
38		}
39		_ = syscall.Kill(-cmd.Process.Pid, syscall.SIGKILL)
40	}

16		"time"
17	
18		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
19	)
20	
21	const (
22		executorPdeathsigHelperEnv       = "DDX_EXECUTOR_PDEATHSIG_HELPER"
23		executorPdeathsigChildPIDFileEnv = "DDX_EXECUTOR_PDEATHSIG_CHILD_PID_FILE"
24		executorPdeathsigGrandPIDFileEnv = "DDX_EXECUTOR_PDEATHSIG_GRANDCHILD_PID_FILE"
25	)
26	
27	func executorReadPIDFile(t *testing.T, path string) int {
28		t.Helper()
29		data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
30		require.NoError(t, err)
31		pid, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(string(data)))
32		require.NoError(t, err)
33		return pid
34	}
35	
36	func executorProcessAlive(pid int) bool {
37		if pid <= 0 {
38			return false
39		}
40		err := syscall.Kill(pid, 0)
41		return err == nil
42	}
43	
44	func executorProcessNotOrphaned(pid int) bool {
45		if pid <= 0 {
46			return true
47		}
48		if err := syscall.Kill(pid, 0); err != nil {
49			return errors.Is(err, syscall.ESRCH)
50		}
51		ppid, ok := readOrphanHarnessParentPID(filepath.Join("/proc", strconv.Itoa(pid), "stat"))
52		if !ok {
53			return false
54		}
55		return ppid != 1
56	}
57	
58	func TestCmdSetProcessGroupLinuxSetsPdeathsigAndSetpgid(t *testing.T) {
59		cmd := exec.Command("sleep", "60")
60		cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd)
61	
62		require.NotNil(t, cmd.SysProcAttr)
63		require.True(t, cmd.SysProcAttr.Setpgid, "expected process-group isolation")
64		require.Equal(t, syscall.SIGKILL, cmd.SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig, "expected Linux parent-death signal")
65	}
66	
67	func TestExecutorLinuxPdeathsigKillsHarnessOnParentSIGKILL(t *testing.T) {
68		dir := t.TempDir()
69		childPIDFile := filepath.Join(dir, "child.pid")
70		grandPIDFile := filepath.Join(dir, "grandchild.pid")
71	
72		ctx := withExecutionEnv(context.Background(), map[string]string{
73			executorPdeathsigHelperEnv:       "1",
74			executorPdeathsigChildPIDFileEnv: childPIDFile,
75			executorPdeathsigGrandPIDFileEnv: grandPIDFile,
76		})
77	
78		executor := &OSExecutor{}
79		result, err := executor.ExecuteInDir(
80			ctx,
81			os.Args[0],
82			[]string{"-test.run=^TestExecutorLinuxPdeathsigKillsHarnessHelper$"},
83			"",
84			dir,
85		)
86		require.NoError(t, err)
87		require.NotNil(t, result)
88	
89		childPID := executorReadPIDFile(t, childPIDFile)
90		grandPID := executorReadPIDFile(t, grandPIDFile)
91	
92		require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
93			return !executorProcessAlive(childPID) && !executorProcessAlive(grandPID)
94		}, 5*time.Second, 25*time.Millisecond, "parent-death cleanup must reap the harness tree")
95	
96		require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
97			return executorProcessNotOrphaned(grandPID)
98		}, 5*time.Second, 25*time.Millisecond, "grandchild must not stick around as a PID 1 orphan")
99	}
100	
101	func TestExecutorLinuxPdeathsigKillsHarnessHelper(t *testing.T) {
102		if os.Getenv(executorPdeathsigHelperEnv) != "1" {
103			return
104		}
105	
106		childPIDFile := os.Getenv(executorPdeathsigChildPIDFileEnv)
107		grandPIDFile := os.Getenv(executorPdeathsigGrandPIDFileEnv)
108		if childPIDFile == "" || grandPIDFile == "" {
109			t.Fatal("helper pid file env vars are required")
110		}
111	
112		grandchild := exec.Command("sleep", "300")
113		cmdSetProcessGroup(grandchild)
114		require.NoError(t, grandchild.Start())
115	
116		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(childPIDFile, []byte(strconv.Itoa(os.Getpid())+"\n"), 0o644))
117		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(grandPIDFile, []byte(strconv.Itoa(grandchild.Process.Pid)+"\n"), 0o644))
118	
119		// Give the outer test time to observe the spawned tree before we
120		// simulate a worker crash with SIGKILL.
121		time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
122		_ = syscall.Kill(os.Getpid(), syscall.SIGKILL)
123	}
124	
125	func TestExecutorStartParentDeathSignalThreadGuard(t *testing.T) {
126		_, file, _, ok := runtime.Caller(0)
127		require.True(t, ok)
128	
129		data, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(file), "executor.go"))
130		require.NoError(t, err)
131	
132		src := string(data)
133		require.Contains(t, src, "runtime.LockOSThread()", "start path must lock the OS thread before cmd.Start()")
134		require.Contains(t, src, "cmd.Start()", "start path must still start the child process under the lock")
135	}
136	
137	func TestExecutorGracefulSignalStillTerminatesHarnessTree(t *testing.T) {
138		dir := t.TempDir()
139		childPIDFile := filepath.Join(dir, "child.pid")
140		grandPIDFile := filepath.Join(dir, "grandchild.pid")
141		scriptPath := filepath.Join(dir, "harness.sh")
142		script := `#!/bin/sh
143	set -eu
144	sleep 300 &
145	echo $$ > "` + childPIDFile + `"
146	echo $! > "` + grandPIDFile + `"
147	wait
148	`
149		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(scriptPath, []byte(script), 0o755))
150	
151		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
152		time.AfterFunc(100*time.Millisecond, cancel)
153	
154		executor := &OSExecutor{}
155		result, err := executor.ExecuteInDir(ctx, scriptPath, nil, "", dir)
156		require.Error(t, err, "graceful cancellation should stop the harness tree")
157		require.NotNil(t, result)
158	
159		childPID := executorReadPIDFile(t, childPIDFile)
160		grandPID := executorReadPIDFile(t, grandPIDFile)
161		require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
162			return !executorProcessAlive(childPID) && !executorProcessAlive(grandPID)
163		}, 5*time.Second, 25*time.Millisecond, "graceful cancellation must kill the entire harness tree")
164	}

diff --git a/cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend_unix_test.go b/cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend_unix_test.go
index e41be5e8a..aea44fdd0 100644
--- a/cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend_unix_test.go
+++ b/cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend_unix_test.go
@@ -7,6 +7,7 @@ import (
 	"path/filepath"
 	"syscall"
 	"testing"
+	"time"
 
 	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
 )
@@ -22,3 +23,65 @@ func TestDockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup(t *testing.T) {
 	require.Contains(t, cmd.Args, "--rm")
 	require.Contains(t, cmd.Args, "--init")
 }
+
+func TestDockerCloneBackendConfiguresProcessGroupForDockerCommand(t *testing.T) {
+	// Verify the docker-clone backend applies the same process-group setup contract
+	// to the docker invocation as other execute-bead harness paths.
+	cmd := dockerAttemptCommand(context.Background(), "ps")
+
+	require.NotNil(t, cmd)
+	require.NotNil(t, cmd.SysProcAttr, "docker command must have SysProcAttr configured")
+	require.True(t, cmd.SysProcAttr.Setpgid, "docker command must set Setpgid for process-group isolation")
+	require.Equal(t, syscall.SIGKILL, cmd.SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig, "docker command must set Pdeathsig for parent-death cleanup")
+}
+
+func TestDockerCloneBackendTerminatesContainerOnWorkerCancellation(t *testing.T) {
+	// Verify context cancellation or graceful worker shutdown stops the docker CLI process
+	// and the associated containerized harness tree.
+	// Note: This test verifies the mechanism without requiring an actual docker daemon.
+	// The actual container cleanup is guaranteed by the --rm flag in dockerRunArgs.
+
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
+
+	// Create a docker command that will be cancelled
+	cmd := dockerAttemptCommand(ctx, "ps")
+
+	// Cancel the context to simulate worker shutdown
+	time.AfterFunc(10*time.Millisecond, cancel)
+
+	// The command should respect context cancellation through exec.CommandContext
+	err := cmd.Run()
+
+	// We expect an error since we cancelled the context
+	require.Error(t, err, "docker command should be interrupted by context cancellation")
+
+	// Verify the context was actually cancelled
+	require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
+		return ctx.Err() != nil
+	}, 100*time.Millisecond, 5*time.Millisecond, "context should be cancelled")
+}
+
+func TestDockerCloneBackendParentDeathCleanupDocumentedOrEnforced(t *testing.T) {
+	// Verify abnormal parent-death behavior for docker-clone is either enforced
+	// through parent-death/process-group/container cleanup or has a documented justification.
+
+	cmd := dockerAttemptCommand(context.Background(), "ps")
+
+	// Verify that process-group setup is in place for docker command
+	require.NotNil(t, cmd.SysProcAttr, "docker command must have SysProcAttr")
+	require.True(t, cmd.SysProcAttr.Setpgid, "Setpgid must be set for process-group isolation")
+	require.Equal(t, syscall.SIGKILL, cmd.SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig, "Pdeathsig must be set to SIGKILL for parent-death cleanup")
+
+	// Verify the docker run arguments include --rm for automatic container cleanup
+	ws := &AttemptWorkspace{
+		ProjectRoot: "/repo",
+		WorkDir:     "/tmp/work",
+		BeadID:      "ddx-test",
+		AttemptID:   "test-attempt",
+		DockerRun:   "/tmp/runtime",
+		DockerHome:  "/tmp/runtime/home",
+	}
+	args := dockerRunArgs(nil, ws, "/usr/bin/ddx", "test:latest", nil)
+	require.Contains(t, args, "--rm", "docker run must include --rm for automatic container cleanup on exit")
+	require.Contains(t, args, "--init", "docker run must include --init for proper signal handling inside container")
+}
